abilities.name: PheroBlast
abilities.description: Scout ants release a concentrated phero-burst, blowing the fog off everything within smelling distance.

abilities.name: Ant Plane-Switch
abilities.description: An ant-mage hauls the column up onto another plane via short-range pheromone-thread teleport. Once per scenario, consumes the party's order for that turn.

abilities.name: Spider Corner-Cross
abilities.description: Spiders are at home crawling around corners. When a spider party's move would land on a plane edge, the engine automatically follows the corner adjacency in the same step. Faction-wide passive; no order required.

abilities.name: Boat-Form
abilities.description: Worker ants link mandibles and legs into a buoyant raft. Stay low, stay dry, stay alive.

abilities.name: Apply Royal Jelly
abilities.description: A dose of the good stuff -- Her Majesty's blessing in jar form. Stiffens the carapace and sharpens the bite, briefly.

abilities.name: Wrath of the Queen
abilities.description: The Queen unleashes the full fury of the colony. Everything in range is forcibly reminded who runs this bathroom.

abilities.name: Volley
abilities.description: Archer ants loose a coordinated salvo of seed-tipped arrows. Pre-battle softening, the textbook way -- and a heavy chassis target like the Spider Queen catches the worst of it.

abilities.name: Brace
abilities.description: Defenders lock shields and dig in. Nothing's getting through — not on their watch.

abilities.name: Mend
abilities.description: The medic patches up the squad with sap-salve and stern words. Walk it off, soldier.

abilities.name: Scout Ping
abilities.description: A quick antenna-twitch reveals what's hiding just past the next tile. Standard recon, on tap. (Suppressed at night.)

abilities.name: Web Tangle
abilities.description: Spiders fling a sticky line across the path. Whoever steps in is going nowhere fast — and swings half-mast while the silk drags on their joints.

abilities.name: Web-Mend
abilities.description: The Spider Queen weaves silk into her wounds between exchanges. Slow bleeders close while she still has thread to spare.

abilities.name: Web-Snare
abilities.description: When the silk runs thin and her wounds won't close, the Spider Queen flings the last strand as a snare. One attacker is bound for a single beat — long enough, if the timing is right.

abilities.name: Spin Web
abilities.description: The spinner spends a turn weaving silk across the current tile. Any ant party that steps on it must pause for a turn while it tears free; the web breaks afterward.

abilities.name: Recruit
abilities.description: An ant mage attempts pheromone-mediated diplomacy on a single isolated foe. 25% of the time the target switches sides; otherwise nothing happens (mage doesn't attack this turn).

abilities.name: Spawn Spiderlings
abilities.description: Spider-queen disgorges a brood — 5 tiny spiderlings scatter from her tile as independent parties. Once per scenario; they're fragile but a real distraction.

abilities.name: Magic Arrow
abilities.description: A mage charges an arrow with phero-magic and an archer fires it. Single target, ranged, hits hard. Requires at least 1 mage and 1 archer in the party.

abilities.name: Hypnotize
abilities.description: Any spider unit may attempt to hypnotize a neutral party at the same tile. Costs the caster half its current HP (rounded down). 80% chance to seize control of the neutral party for 5-10 turns; on failure a stinkbug target leaves a damage zone.

abilities.name: Phalanx Charge
abilities.description: Two or more footmen lock shields and barrel forward as one. Hits every enemy in the target party for moderate damage.

abilities.name: Venom Blast
abilities.description: A spider-spinner or spider-queen sprays a fan of venom across the enemy front rank — burning silk-soaked fangs and brittle armor. Hits every living unit in the targeted rank for heavy damage. Reserved for combos in later turns.

abilities.name: Royal Onslaught
abilities.description: Mage and worker channel the Queen's blessing into a single thunderous strike. All enemy units in the target party take heavy damage.

abilities.name: Venom Storm
abilities.description: Spinner and queen weave venom and silk into a saturating cloud — every unit in the target party is poisoned and bound at once.

classes.name: Ant Queen
classes.description: The colony's monarch — her presence galvanizes every soldier within earshot.

classes.name: Spider Queen
classes.description: Eight-legged matriarch whose web-borne wrath terrifies trespassers.

classes.name: Sergeant
classes.description: Veteran field commander who keeps the line steady and the orders loud.

classes.name: Scout Captain
classes.description: Forward-deployed pathfinder who reads the battlefield before it reads her.

classes.name: War Chief
classes.description: All-out aggressor who trades caution for crushing offense.

classes.name: Quartermaster
classes.description: Logistics specialist who keeps the party fed, jellied, and patched up.

formations.name: Phalanx
formations.description: Three or more ants lock mandibles and shields into a tight wedge. No extra punch, but a hell of a wall — fragile ants survive longer when shoulder to shoulder.

formations.name: Swarm
formations.description: Four or more ants pile onto a single target in a coordinated rush. Maximum bite, minimum caution — the textbook ant glass-cannon play.

formations.name: Lift
formations.description: Five or more workers hoist something heavy together — a crumb, a pebble, a wounded comrade. Slight defensive boost while the load is shared, and a hook for future cargo and rescue mechanics.

formations.name: Skirmish Line
formations.description: Three or more archers space out and stagger their volleys. Sustained ranged pressure rewards parties that lean into Volley spam.

inventory.name: Whiskers the Mouse Mercenary
inventory.description: A grumpy, scarred house-mouse for hire. Confirmed F.N.G. (Friendly New Giant). Restricted to the floor plane and demands payment up front. One-time hire only — Mr. Gristle insists he 'knows a guy.'

inventory.name: Sugar Poultice (Field Pack)
inventory.description: A crumb of damp sugar wrapped in spider-silk gauze. Restores minor HP to a single unit. Standard issue M.R.E. (Medical Restorative Edible).

inventory.name: Sugar Poultice (Care Package)
inventory.description: A whole sugar grain. Heavily restores HP to a single unit. Grasshopper guarantees 'pre-licked, ant-grade, hospital-clean.'

inventory.name: Honeydew Tincture
inventory.description: A field-applied party-wide pick-me-up brewed from aphid honeydew. Restores HP across the whole party. Smells faintly of regret.

inventory.name: Caffeine Crystal
inventory.description: Scraped from beneath the coffee mug ring. Temporarily boosts a unit's Agility. Side effects include the jitters and bad decisions.

inventory.name: Capsaicin Dram
inventory.description: A drop of hot-sauce off the kitchen tile. Temporarily boosts a unit's Attack. Mr. Gristle warns: 'Do not, repeat, DO NOT touch your antennae after handling.'

inventory.name: Thimble Helmet
inventory.description: A genuine sewing-room thimble, refitted for ant-skull anatomy. Permanently raises Armor on the equipped unit. Mr. Gristle calls it 'P.P.E. — Personal Protective Exoskeleton.'

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: Listen up, soldiers — eyes front, mandibles shut. Theater of operations: one (1) bathroom, three (3) planes, five (5) P.O.S.T.s — that's Position Of Strategic interesT, and yes, we will be saying it like that. The Queen holds at the Storm Drain; lose her, lose the war, end of S.O.P. Tangoes — that's our spiders — are dug in at the Web on the ceiling and they will not, repeat WILL NOT, leave it without a reason. Expect scout elements probing toward the S.O.A.P. — Salient Objective: Anchor Point — that's the soap dish, people, keep up. Hostile response packages will rally on plane transitions, so the Towel Rack and Wall Crack are the choke we own or the choke we die on. Move with purpose, capture with prejudice, and remember: in this army we don't retreat, we conduct R.A.A.E. — Rapid Antward Adjustment of Engagement. HOO-RAH.

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: Web is down, Queen — I say again, web is DOWN. Spider Queen has bent the knee and is, as we speak, signing a non-aggression-stroke-fealty pact in eight-legged cursive. We took the bathroom, ma'am. We TOOK the bathroom. Mark this date on the colony calendar: Day One of Operation Whole Damn House. Stand down, drink your honeydew, and write your larvae. HOO-RAH.

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: ...Queen is down. Queen is down. Storm Drain has fallen. All units, sound off where you can — the rest, lay your antennae and rest easy. We were ants. We were ARMY. The bathroom remembers. Antonio, out.

lines.speaker: Cpl. Bicep
lines.text: First contact, first contact! Tangoes on the tile, formation up — for the Queen, for the colony, for the GODDAMN crumb!

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: P.O.S.T. secured! That's one for the colony, zero for the eight-leggers. Plant the pheromone, garrison up, and start the H.A.R. — Healing And Recovery — clock. Outstanding work, soldier. Outstanding.

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: Queen's ultimate is GREEN, repeat, GREEN. C.A.N. — Charge At Nominal — full bar, ma'am. Permission to suggest, with all due respect, that you absolutely positively melt somebody with it. At your discretion, my Queen.

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: MOTHER OF MANDIBLES — you SEEING this?! Battlefield is CLEAR, ma'am, the tangoes are jelly, the JELLY is jelly. That is what we in the corps call a Queen-Level Event, and I am writing a song about it tonight.

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: Leader down, leader DOWN — party is L.O.B., Leaderless On Battlefield. They're falling back to base on autopilot, rate-of-march reduced, and they will NOT take new orders until command is restored at the Storm Drain. We do not abandon ours. Get them home.

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: Logistics check, my Queen — R.J.S. is in the red. That's Royal Jelly Supply, and forward elements are running on fumes and patriotism. Recommend we run a J.U.G. — Jelly Update Gallop — back to the Drain before our buff bar goes flatline.

lines.speaker: Cpl. Bicep
lines.text: When I get home, I'm gonna eat a whole sugar grain. Whole. Grain. By myself. That's a promise to me from me.

lines.speaker: Pvt. Snitz
lines.text: Sarge says the floor is called the A.O. — Area of Operations. I asked what the ceiling's called. He said 'the ceiling.' I do not understand this army.

lines.speaker: Pvt. Grub
lines.text: I joined up for the dental. Turns out we don't have teeth. Turns out we don't have dental. Outstanding.

lines.speaker: Sgt. Antonio
lines.text: Steady on the line, ladies. We hold what we hold, we take what we take, and we do NOT, repeat NOT, stare at the soap dish too long — it's slippery and it's a metaphor.

templates.name: Mead
templates.description: Heals every unit in the party to full HP. Single use.

templates.name: Scout Lens
templates.description: Party-wide +1 agility (initiative). Reserved for vision once the mechanic exists.

templates.name: Royal Jelly Vial
templates.description: Adds +2 jelly doses to the picking-up party (capped at jelly capacity).

beats.title: The web tightens
beats.message: Distant skittering carries down through the pipes — the spider queen has noticed your approach.

beats.title: The Wall Crack splinters
beats.message: Dust falls. Light spills through the fracture. The colony's reach now extends to the east wall.
